product.purpose: Let delivery managers plan, track, and unblock multi-team project delivery so commitments are met and risk is surfaced early.
product.regulatory_sensitivity: low-to-moderate (internal financial and resourcing data, no regulated PII beyond employee records)

goals: see the true status of every active project at a glance
identify blocked and at-risk work before it slips
produce a defensible status narrative for stakeholders
find what to work on next
update task state and log progress with minimal friction
understand portfolio health without learning the tool
drill into a specific at-risk initiative

workflows: Triage the daily portfolio: scan all active projects, sort by risk, open the worst offenders.
Resolve a blocker: open a blocked task, read context, reassign or escalate, record the decision.
Replan a sprint or milestone: drag work across dates/owners and confirm capacity is not exceeded.
Generate a status report: filter to a project, summarise progress, flag risks, export or share.
Cross-link dependencies: connect a task to the upstream work it waits on and surface the chain.

environment.secondary_device: laptop; occasional tablet for read-only review
environment.connectivity: stable corporate network or home broadband; offline not expected

risks: Stale or misleading status causes a missed commitment to surface too late.
Density overwhelms newer or occasional users (executives), who then distrust the data.
A fast bulk edit (drag-replan, multi-assign) silently violates capacity or overwrites another manager's change.
Filtered/saved views hide work, creating false confidence that nothing is at risk.
Notification volume trains managers to ignore genuinely urgent escalations.
constraints: Primary screens must remain legible and scannable at high information density.
Destructive or wide-reaching edits must be reversible or clearly confirmed.
Keyboard-first navigation expected by power users for daily throughput.
Must integrate with existing SSO and corporate identity.
verified: The product is an internal B2B project-management application (stated in the brief).
The primary user is a delivery manager who uses it daily.
Information density is high and the primary device is desktop.
Criticality of accurate status is high for this user.
inferred: Power users will expect keyboard-first navigation and saved views, based on daily expert use.
Executives interact rarely and need a lower-density entry point, inferred from the stakeholder role.
Replanning involves bulk edits whose blast radius must be controlled, inferred from manager goals.
assumptions: Sessions happen on a stable network; offline support is out of scope.
English-only localisation is acceptable for the initial audience.
Capacity/resourcing data exists and can back a 'do not over-allocate' guardrail.
SSO is the expected authentication model.
unresolved: Exact team and portfolio sizes (tens vs thousands of tasks) that drive virtualization needs.
Whether status is human-entered, integration-fed, or both, which changes how much to trust it.
Whether regulated client data ever lands in project fields (would raise regulatory_sensitivity).
Accessibility obligations of the buying organisations.
evidence: task-brief: delivery manager, daily, high criticality, desktop-primary, high density
inference-only: no telemetry, interviews, or repository access were available at authoring time
